sections.heading: Medically Operable, Low-Risk Features
sections.content: Branching basis: Surgical operability, Cytoreduction achievability, Histology (epithelioid), Absence of high-risk features. Criteria: Patient is medically operable, complete cytoreduction is achievable, and has low-risk features (defined as epithelioid histology and absence of ANY high-risk features). Options: [Cytoreductive surgery (CRS) + hyperthermic intraperitoneal (IP) chemotherapy (HIPEC) - Preferred - Category 2A - linked regimen IDs: PEM-C_HIPEC_Cis/Dox, PEM-C_HIPEC_Cis, PEM-C_HIPEC_Carbo, PEM-C_HIPEC_Cis/Mito]. Adjuvant: If no high-risk features are identified post-surgery, imaging surveillance is sufficient.

sections.heading: Medically Operable, High-Risk Features
sections.content: Branching basis: Surgical operability, Cytoreduction achievability, Presence of ANY high-risk feature. Criteria: Patient is medically operable and complete cytoreduction is achievable, but presence of ANY surgical/pathologic high-risk feature (biphasic/sarcomatoid histology, nodal metastasis, Ki-67 >9%, thrombocytosis, PS=2, bicavitary disease, high disease burden/PCI >17, CC >1). Options: [Cytoreductive surgery (CRS) + hyperthermic intraperitoneal (IP) chemotherapy (HIPEC) - Preferred - Category 2A - linked regimen IDs: PEM-C_HIPEC_Cis/Dox, PEM-C_HIPEC_Cis, PEM-C_HIPEC_Carbo, PEM-C_HIPEC_Cis/Mito]. Adjuvant: If high-risk features are identified post-surgery, adjuvant systemic therapy is recommended.

sections.heading: Medically Inoperable or Complete Cytoreduction Not Achievable
sections.content: Branching basis: Surgical operability, Cytoreduction achievability. Criteria 1: Patient is medically inoperable, complete cytoreduction is not achievable, OR presence of ANY high-risk feature precluding surgery. Options 1: [Systemic therapy - Preferred - Category 2A - linked regimen IDs: PEM-D_Epi_1L_Pref, PEM-D_BiSar_1L_Pref]. Criteria 2: Subsequent evaluation after initial systemic therapy shows patient is now medically operable and complete cytoreduction is achievable. Options 2: [Consider CRS + HIPEC - Other recommended - Category 2A - linked regimen IDs: PEM-C_HIPEC_Cis/Dox, PEM-C_HIPEC_Cis, PEM-C_HIPEC_Carbo, PEM-C_HIPEC_Cis/Mito; Imaging surveillance - Preferred - Category 2A - no linked regimens]. Adjuvant: N/A.

sections.heading: Progression After Prior CRS+HIPEC
sections.content: Branching basis: Prior treatment history, Disease progression. Criteria: Patient has progression of disease after prior CRS and HIPEC. Options: [Systemic therapy - Preferred - Category 2A - linked regimen IDs: PEM-D_Epi_Sub_Pref, PEM-D_BiSar_Sub_Pref; Repeat CRS + HIPEC (if >12 months from prior procedure and otherwise operable) - Useful in certain circumstances - Category 2A - linked regimen IDs: PEM-C_HIPEC_Cis/Dox, PEM-C_HIPEC_Cis, PEM-C_HIPEC_Carbo, PEM-C_HIPEC_Cis/Mito]. Adjuvant: N/A.

sections.heading: Systemic Therapy for Biphasic or Sarcomatoid Histology
sections.content: Branching basis: Histology (Biphasic/Sarcomatoid). Criteria: Patient has biphasic or sarcomatoid peritoneal mesothelioma and requires systemic therapy (e.g., medically inoperable, high-risk features). Options: [Ipilimumab + Nivolumab - Preferred - Category 2A - linked regimen IDs: PEM-D_IpiNivo; (Carboplatin or Cisplatin)/Pemetrexed + Bevacizumab - Other recommended - Category 2A - linked regimen IDs: PEM-D_PemPlatBev; (Carboplatin or Cisplatin)/Pemetrexed - Other recommended - Category 2A - linked regimen IDs: PEM-D_PemPlat]. Adjuvant: N/A.
